CREATE TABLE test_array (id INT, values ARRAY<INT>);
SELECT id, array_length(values) AS length FROM test_array;
SELECT id FROM test_array WHERE array_length(values) > 2;
SELECT id, (SELECT array_length(values) FROM test_array WHERE id = 1) AS length_of_id_1;
SELECT id, array_length(values) + 1 AS adjusted_length FROM test_array;
SELECT t1.id, array_length(t1.values) AS length, t2.other_column FROM test_array t1 JOIN (SELECT 1 AS other_column) t2 ON t1.id = t2.other_column;
WITH temp AS (SELECT id, array_length(values) AS len FROM test_array) SELECT id FROM temp WHERE len = (SELECT MAX(len) FROM temp);
SELECT id FROM test_array WHERE values IS NOT NULL AND array_length(values) >= 0;
SELECT array_length(array_length(values) * 2) FROM test_array WHERE id = 2;
